items.question: ___ student in the class passed the exam.
explanations: 'All student' is wrong; 'all' takes a plural noun.

items.question: ___ of my parents are doctors.
explanations: Correct! 'Both' is for exactly two: 'Both of my parents'.
'Every' takes a singular noun and verb.
'Each of my parents' would need 'is', not 'are'.

items.question: ___ the tickets have sold out.
explanations: Correct! 'All' + plural noun + plural verb: 'All the tickets have'.
'Every ticket' would need a singular verb.
'Each ticket' would need a singular verb.

items.question: Each of them ___ a different opinion.
explanations: Correct! 'Each' takes a singular verb: 'Each of them has'.
'Having' can't be the main verb here.

items.question: There's a chair on ___ side of the table. (two sides)
explanations: 'All side' is wrong; 'all' takes a plural noun.

items.question: ___ room in the hotel is fully booked.
explanations: Correct! 'Every' + singular noun + singular verb: 'Every room is'.
'All room' is wrong; use 'all rooms are'.

items.question: I've read ___ of these books and enjoyed them. (three books)
explanations: Correct! 'All of these books' — three or more.
'Each one is' doesn't fit this sentence.

items.question: Correct this: 'Every students are welcome.'
explanations: Correct! 'Every' + singular noun + singular verb: 'Every student is'.
'Every student are' uses a plural verb.
The original has two number errors.

items.question: Correct this: 'Both my three sisters live abroad.'
options: All my three sisters live abroad.
Both my three sisters live abroad.
Every my three sisters live abroad.
Each my three sisters live abroad.
explanations: Correct! For three, use 'all': 'All my three sisters'.

items.question: Which words take a SINGULAR verb?
explanations: Correct! 'Every' and 'each' take a singular noun and verb.
